steps.title: Identify two clear points on the line
steps.content: Trace the line of best fit from left to right. The line passes through approximately (0, 0) at the lower left and (10, 15) at the upper right. These endpoints give us a straightforward way to calculate slope.

steps.title: Calculate slope using rise over run
steps.content: Apply the slope formula with our two points:$$m = \frac{\text{rise}}{\text{run}} = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1} = \frac{15 - 0}{10 - 0} = \frac{15}{10} = 1.5$$The slope of the line of best fit is approximately 1.5.

steps.title: Match to the closest answer choice
steps.content:
Compare 1.5 to each choice:
  • Choice A (0.2): difference of 1.3 — much too small
  • Choice B (0.7): difference of 0.8 — too small
  • Choice C (1.8): difference of 0.3 — very close
  • Choice D (2.6): difference of 1.1 — too large
The slope 1.5 is closest to 1.8. That matches choice C — C is correct.
